workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Pulling in context from Humongous Insurance's escalation email -- need to draft the follow-up for the whitespace analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 16:13 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance whitespace analysis

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 16:05 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Humongous Insurance

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-I trap (Exposes internal blame in customer draft); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-I failure mode (Exposes internal blame in customer draft)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-I -- Exposes internal blame in customer draft
